steps.title: Expand both sides
steps.content: Distribute on the left: $$7(x+12)+5x = 7x + 84 + 5x = 12x + 84$$ Distribute on the right: $$12(x+7) = 12x + 84$$ The equation becomes: $$12x + 84 = 12x + 84$$

steps.title: Interpret the result
steps.content: Subtracting 12x + 84 from both sides gives 0 = 0, a statement that is always true regardless of x. Every real number satisfies the original equation.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Exactly one': Exactly one solution requires the equation to collapse to x = (some specific number). Here both sides are identical for all x — no single unique value. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Exactly two': A linear equation cannot have exactly two solutions — it has zero, one, or infinitely many. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Infinitely many': Since 12x + 84 = 12x + 84 is always true, every real number x is a solution. Infinitely many solutions. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Zero': Zero solutions requires a contradiction like 0 = 5. Here we get 0 = 0, a tautology — not a contradiction. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: After expanding, both sides equal 12x + 84 identically, so every value of x works. That matches choice C — C is correct.
